iterations.hypothesis: Initial: threshold-crossing taker using depletion score. score>0.3 → BUY, score<-0.3 → SELL. SL/TP + signal-reversion exit at exit_threshold=0.1. Cooldown=50 ticks.
iterations.analysis: Massively overtrading: 27,060 fills, 50 fills/minute. Signal-reversion exit fires every few ticks (score bounces 0.1-0.3). No pending-entry guard — during the 5ms+1ms order latency, position=0, so new entries fire every tick. Fees of $0.25 destroyed PnL.
iterations.next_action: Add _pending_order flag to prevent in-flight duplicates. Remove signal exit, use SL/TP only. EWMA-smooth the score (alpha=0.1). Cooldown=200.

iterations.hypothesis: Add EWMA score smoothing (alpha=0.1) and _pending_order guard to prevent in-flight duplicates. Remove signal exit, use SL/TP only. Cooldown=200.
iterations.analysis: Even more fills (47,470). _pending_close was only applied to exits, not entries. During 5ms entry latency, position=0 and cooldown already passed → multiple entries fire simultaneously. Need _pending_order to gate ALL intents.
iterations.next_action: Unify _pending_close and _pending_entry into single _pending_order flag gating all intents. Reset in on_execution_report.

iterations.hypothesis: Unified _pending_order flag gates all intents. Reset on every execution report. EWMA alpha=0.1. SL/TP exit only.
iterations.analysis: Trade count now controlled (61 fills, ~6.8/hr, spread across hours 7-15). Still negative: buying at $80,127 avg, selling at $80,077. EWMA alpha=0.1 lags signal by ~10 ticks; by the time threshold is reached, the price move has already happened. Need faster signal.
iterations.next_action: Increase EWMA alpha to 0.7 (faster signal). Add signal zero-crossing exit (score < 0 for longs). Raise entry_threshold to 0.4 to filter noise.

iterations.hypothesis: Faster EWMA (alpha=0.7), zero-crossing exit, entry_threshold=0.4, cooldown=100.
iterations.analysis: Zero-crossing exit + fast alpha causes 3,884 fills (~1,942 round trips). Score flips rapidly with alpha=0.7 → frequent zero crossings → many exits → cooldown resets → re-entries. Still negative: buying at avg price higher than selling. Signal direction appears contrarian — positive score (buy pressure) → price mean-reverts DOWN. Should sell on positive score, buy on negative.
iterations.next_action: Flip entry direction: score>threshold → SELL SHORT, score<-threshold → BUY. SL/TP only exit (no zero-crossing), cooldown=1000 ticks.

iterations.hypothesis: Contrarian direction: positive score (buy pressure) → SELL (mean reversion). Negative score → BUY. SL/TP only exit. Long cooldown=1000 ticks.
changes: Flipped entry direction: positive score → ASK (sell), negative → BID (buy)
iterations.analysis: First profitable iteration. Contrarian direction confirmed: after strong buy sweeps, price mean-reverts. 101 fills (~50 round trips), pct_positive_buckets=0.80. Sharpe=0.005 still far from target 1.0 due to small edge per trade ($0.000213 / 50 trades = $0.0000043/trade). Need to optimize entry_threshold and SL/TP to capture larger moves per trade.
iterations.next_action: Remote sweep: entry_threshold=[0.3,0.4,0.5], stop_loss_bps=[8,12,15], take_profit_bps=[12,18,25], cooldown_ticks=[500,1000,2000] — 81 jobs to find best param combo.

iterations.hypothesis: Sweep entry_threshold=[0.3,0.4,0.5] x stop_loss_bps=[8,12,15] x take_profit_bps=[12,18,25] x cooldown_ticks=[500,1000,2000] (81 jobs) to find optimal param combo for contrarian taker.
iterations.analysis: 22/81 jobs profitable. Best: entry=0.3, sl=8, tp=12, cd=500 → sharpe=0.032, pct_pos=0.80, 145 fills. Tight SL/TP dominates — confirms mean-reversion: moves are small and quick. entry=0.3 > 0.4 > 0.5. cooldown=500 > 1000 > 2000 (more trades = better). Sharpe improved 6x from iter 5 (0.005 → 0.032) but still 31x from target 1.0. Notable: entry=0.3,sl=15,tp=25,cd=500 gets pct_pos=1.00 with 49 fills.
iterations.next_action: Fine sweep around winner: entry=[0.25,0.3,0.35] x sl=[5,6,8] x tp=[8,10,12] x cd=[300,500,750] (81 jobs).

iterations.hypothesis: Fine-grid sweep around iter_006 winner: entry=[0.25,0.3,0.35] x sl=[5,6,8] x tp=[8,10,12] x cd=[300,500,750] (81 jobs) to find optimal params in tighter neighborhood.
iterations.analysis: 5/81 jobs profitable. All winning jobs share sl=8 and cd=500 — tight SL and moderate cooldown are non-negotiable. sl=5/6 and cd=300/750 uniformly harmful. entry=0.25 slightly better than 0.30 and 0.35. tp=12 best (tp=10 worse, tp=8 much worse). Only 19% improvement from iter_006 (0.032→0.038). Signal is narrow: one profitable pocket. Sharpe plateau at ~0.04, target 1.0 is 26x away.
iterations.next_action: Try structural change: add time-of-day session filter (skip early morning hours where signal may be noisier). Best params: entry=0.25, sl=8, tp=12, cd=500.

iterations.hypothesis: Add session_start_hour=9 filter (skip hours 07-09) to remove noisy low-volume morning entries, keeping only active-session signals. Best params from iter_007 otherwise.
iterations.analysis: Session filter hurt: sharpe 0.038→0.034, pct_positive_buckets 0.80→0.60. 18 fewer fills (139→121). Morning hours 07-09 contributed 25 fills that were net profitable — skipping them removed good trades. Hypothesis was wrong: losing time buckets are NOT in the morning. Session filter approach rejected.
iterations.next_action: Revert session filter. Try pure TradeImbalance signal (w_imbalance=1.0, others=0) to test whether staleness/liq_delta components add signal or noise. Lower entry threshold to 0.20.

iterations.hypothesis: Pure TradeImbalance signal (w_imbalance=1.0, w_staleness=0.0, w_liq_delta=0.0) with lower entry threshold 0.20 to test if composite signal components add noise. Best SL/TP/cooldown from iter_007.
iterations.analysis: Pure TradeImbalance worse than composite: sharpe 0.038→0.022. Staleness and LiquidityDelta components DO contribute positively to signal quality. More fills (147) due to lower threshold but PnL per trade lower. Composite signal with original weights (0.5/0.25/0.25) remains best. 3 consecutive iterations with sharpe declining (0.038→0.034→0.022), plateau confirmed. 26x gap to target sharpe=1.0 with no clear structural path — marking stalled.
iterations.next_action: Session stalled. Best config: entry=0.25, sl=8, tp=12, cd=500, composite weights 0.5/0.25/0.25 (iter_007 winner). Signal has real but small edge on this single trading day. To reach sharpe>1.0 would require multi-day data to reduce sampling noise, or a fundamentally stronger signal.

iterations.hypothesis: Extend date range to 3 days (May 13-15) and increase size=100 to validate edge consistency across market days and improve dollar PnL. Keep best params: entry=0.25, sl=8, tp=12, cd=500.
changes: date range: May 13 only -> May 13-15 (3 days)
iterations.analysis: Edge is real and consistent across all 3 days: win rates 50-58%, 2/3 days profitable, total closed-trade PnL=/bin/zsh.28 before fees. Sharpe dropped to 0.0077 (from 0.038 on 1 day) because the 30-bucket time series has more zero-trade hours and inter-hour variance. May 14 (+222 bps uptrend) had the most trades (215 round trips) but still 51% win rate — the contrarian signal works in both trending and ranging markets, just captures different legs. Key: params optimized on 1 day may not be optimal for 3-day window.
iterations.next_action: Remote sweep on 3-day window to find params optimal for longer horizon: entry=[0.20,0.25,0.30] x sl=[6,8,10] x tp=[10,12,15] x cd=[300,500,750] = 81 jobs.
